name: binfmt-support
title: binfmt-support
description:
Support for extra binary formats
The binfmt_misc kernel module, contained in versions 2.1.43 and later of the
Linux kernel, allows system administrators to register interpreters for
various binary formats based on a magic number or their file extension, and
cause the appropriate interpreter to be invoked whenever a matching file is
executed. Think of it as a more flexible version of the #! executable
interpreter mechanism.
.
This package provides an 'update-binfmts' script with which package
maintainers can register interpreters to be used with this module without
having to worry about writing their own init.d scripts, and which sysadmins
can use for a slightly higher-level interface to this module.

commands.name: update-binfmts
commands.description: maintain registry of executable binary formats

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use update-binfmts because Support for extra binary formats.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use update-binfmts as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `update-binfmts [options] --install name path spec` as documented upstream for update-binfmts.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `update-binfmts [options] --remove name path` as documented upstream for update-binfmts.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `update-binfmts [options] --import [name]` as documented upstream for update-binfmts.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `update-binfmts [options] --unimport [name]` as documented upstream for update-binfmts.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair update-binfmts with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `update-binfmts --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: update-binfmts -- maintain registry of executable binary formats
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install binfmt-support. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `update-binfmts [options] --install name path spec`. Further invocations from upstream: `update-binfmts [options] --remove name path`; `update-binfmts [options] --import [name]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Support for extra binary formats.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: update-binfmts -- maintain registry of executable binary formats.
